risinfo.chaptertitle: 7 Non-Preventive Evaluation and Management Services in Outpatient Settings

sect1.title: CPT
sect1.para: Teaching Point: The moderate MDM is supported by all 3 elements, although only 2 are required.

▶An adolescent patient is seen in consultation for increasingly symptomatic mental health problems. The patient's psychologist has requested evaluation and recommendation for treatment, as the patient has not adequately responded to current treatment. The physician reviews the patient's current medications and results of psychological tests. History is obtained mainly from the patient's mother, who is present. After extensive discussion, new medication is prescribed and a follow-up visit is scheduled in 1 week. The physician sends the psychologist a report with findings and recommendations. The physician's total time spent in medical management (not psychotherapy, which would be reported separately, when provided) is 50 minutes.
